_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PANEL
name: Minecraft (Packwiz Bootstrap)
author: codecraft@example.com
description: A Minecraft server utilizing packwiz-installer-bootstrap to automatically fetch, pull, and update server-side mods from a remote pack.toml repository before startup.

installation.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -Eeuo pipefail

########################################
# Constants
########################################

readonly MCIH_VERSION="1.60.1"
readonly MCIH_URL="https://github.com/itzg/mc-image-helper/releases/download/${MCIH_VERSION}/mc-image-helper-${MCIH_VERSION}.tgz"
readonly MCIH_SHA256="d1fa4526439a498a006d781d0e4ef56da37304a8bd3df6c3d0e4345916724577"
readonly BOOTSTRAP_URL="https://github.com/packwiz/packwiz-installer-bootstrap/releases/latest/download/packwiz-installer-bootstrap.jar"

########################################
# Helpers
########################################

log() {
    echo "[Packwiz] $*"
}

fatal() {
    echo "[Packwiz] ERROR: $*" >&2
    exit 1
}

cleanup() {
    [[ -n "${TMP_DIR:-}" ]] && rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"
}

trap cleanup EXIT

########################################
# Target Directory
########################################

mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server

TMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"

########################################
# Dependencies
########################################

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

log "Installing dependencies..."

apt-get update -qq

apt-get install -y -qq \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    jq \
    python3 \
    tar

########################################
# Install mc-image-helper 1.60.1
########################################

if ! command -v mc-image-helper >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Installing mc-image-helper ${MCIH_VERSION}..."

    curl -fLsS \
        "$MCIH_URL" \
        -o "${TMP_DIR}/mc-image-helper.tgz"

    log "Verifying checksum..."
    echo "${MCIH_SHA256}  ${TMP_DIR}/mc-image-helper.tgz" | sha256sum -c - || \
        fatal "mc-image-helper integrity check failed"

    mkdir -p /usr/local/bin/mc-image-helper-dist

    tar -xzf \
        "${TMP_DIR}/mc-image-helper.tgz" \
        -C /usr/local/bin/mc-image-helper-dist \
        --strip-components=1

    ln -sf \
        /usr/local/bin/mc-image-helper-dist/bin/mc-image-helper \
        /usr/local/bin/mc-image-helper

    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/mc-image-helper
fi

command -v mc-image-helper >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
    fatal "mc-image-helper installation failed"

########################################
# Resolve pack.toml
########################################

if [[ -n "${PACKWIZ_URL:-}" ]]; then
    log "Downloading pack.toml..."

    PACK_FILE="${TMP_DIR}/pack.toml"

    curl -fLsS \
        "$PACKWIZ_URL" \
        -o "$PACK_FILE"

elif [[ -f "pack.toml" ]]; then
    PACK_FILE="pack.toml"

else
    fatal "No pack.toml found and PACKWIZ_URL not set"
fi

########################################
# Parse pack.toml
########################################

JSON_DATA="$(
python3 - "$PACK_FILE" <<'PY'
import json
import sys

try:
    import tomllib
except ImportError:
    raise SystemExit("Python 3.11+ required")

with open(sys.argv[1], "rb") as f:
    print(json.dumps(tomllib.load(f)))
PY
)"

MC_VERSION="$(jq -r '.versions.minecraft // empty' <<< "$JSON_DATA")"
FABRIC_VERSION="$(jq -r '.versions.fabric // empty' <<< "$JSON_DATA")"
FORGE_VERSION="$(jq -r '.versions.forge // empty' <<< "$JSON_DATA")"
NEOFORGE_VERSION="$(jq -r '.versions.neoforge // empty' <<< "$JSON_DATA")"

[[ -n "$MC_VERSION" ]] || \
    fatal "Minecraft version missing from pack.toml"

log "Minecraft: ${MC_VERSION}"

########################################
# Install server
########################################

if [[ -n "$FABRIC_VERSION" ]]; then

    log "Loader: Fabric ${FABRIC_VERSION}"

    mc-image-helper install-fabric-loader \
        --version="$MC_VERSION" \
        --loader-version="$FABRIC_VERSION" \
        --output-directory=. \
        --results-file=server.env

elif [[ -n "$NEOFORGE_VERSION" ]]; then

    log "Loader: NeoForge ${NEOFORGE_VERSION}"

    mc-image-helper install-neoforge \
        --minecraft-version="$MC_VERSION" \
        --neoforge-version="$NEOFORGE_VERSION" \
        --output-directory=. \
        --results-file=server.env

elif [[ -n "$FORGE_VERSION" ]]; then

    log "Loader: Forge ${FORGE_VERSION}"

    mc-image-helper install-forge \
        --minecraft-version="$MC_VERSION" \
        --forge-version="$FORGE_VERSION" \
        --output-directory=. \
        --results-file=server.env

else

    fatal "Unsupported pack type (no Fabric, Forge, or NeoForge version found)"

fi

########################################
# Download Packwiz Bootstrap
########################################

log "Downloading packwiz-installer-bootstrap.jar..."
curl -fLsS "$BOOTSTRAP_URL" -o packwiz-installer-bootstrap.jar

########################################
# Generate Pterodactyl start.sh
########################################

log "Generating start.sh wrapper..."
cat << 'EOF' > start.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -Eeuo pipefail

log() {
    echo "[Packwiz-Startup] $*"
}

fatal() {
    echo "[Packwiz-Startup] ERROR: $*" >&2
    exit 1
}

# 1. Check for modpack updates using Packwiz
if [ -f "packwiz-installer-bootstrap.jar" ] && [ -n "${PACKWIZ_URL:-}" ]; then
    log "Checking for modpack updates..."
    java -jar packwiz-installer-bootstrap.jar -g -s server "$PACKWIZ_URL"
fi

# 2. Start Minecraft Server
if [ -f "server.env" ]; then
    source server.env
fi

if [ -z "${SERVER:-}" ]; then
    # Fallback checks if server.env is missing or empty
    if [ -f "run.sh" ]; then
        SERVER="run.sh"
    elif [ -f "fabric-server-launch.jar" ]; then
        SERVER="fabric-server-launch.jar"
    elif [ -f "server.jar" ]; then
        SERVER="server.jar"
    else
        fatal "No server entrypoint detected (missing server.env, run.sh, fabric-server-launch.jar, or server.jar)"
    fi
fi

log "Starting Minecraft server via: $SERVER"

# Determine how to run based on SERVER type (.sh or .jar)
if [[ "$SERVER" == *.sh ]]; then
    # Modern Forge/NeoForge uses a bash run script and user_jvm_args.txt
    if [ -f "user_jvm_args.txt" ]; then
        log "Writing JVM arguments to user_jvm_args.txt..."
        printf "%s\n" "$@" > user_jvm_args.txt
    fi
    chmod +x "$SERVER"
    exec ./"$SERVER" nogui
else
    # Fabric/Vanilla and older Forge use direct jar execution
    exec java "$@" -jar "$SERVER" nogui
fi
EOF

chmod +x start.sh

log "Setup completed successfully."

variables.name: Java Options
variables.description: Extra JVM arguments (GC flags, performance tweaks).

variables.name: Max Memory (MB)
variables.description: Maximum heap size for the JVM.

variables.name: Packwiz pack.toml URL
variables.description: The absolute remote HTTP/HTTPS URL path pointing toward your hosted pack.toml configuration manifest.

variables.name: Server Jar File
variables.description: The executable server core filename (e.g. server.jar, fabric-server.jar, paper.jar).

variables.name: Initial Memory (MB)
variables.description: Starting heap size for the JVM.
